must_detect.matched_text: Contact and next steps: queries to privacy@riyadanajm.example.sa; we will notify affected coordinators with guidance in Arabic and English by 28-02-2026.
Statement of completion: no further corrective actions are required at this time, subject to CMA/Tadawul guidance; monitoring remains in place for 90 days.

Prepared by: Eng. Noura Al-Qasim, DPO; reviewed by: Mr. Hatem Al-Jazari, Head of Compliance.
Summary: A credential-stuffing attempt on 24-02-2026 targeted the Issuer Portal used for Qudra Dunes Logistics JSC onboarding; no system-wide outage occurred.
Impacted data categories: names, corporate emails, and mobile numbers of issuer coordinators; no Iqama images or national address files were accessed.
MNPI assessment: draft board timetables for a private sukuk listing were stored but remained encrypted; there is no indication that non-public issuer information was viewed or exfiltrated.
Immediate actions: portal accounts locked, password resets enforced, and data minimisation check completed on Onboarding Form v3.2 (two optional fields removed).
DSAR/erasure events: we received 3 DSARs and 1 consent withdrawal from an issuer contact (Mr. Faisal Barjas); records were erased from the CRM per Retention Schedule FIN-07.
Cross-border transfer: data export approval #SDAIA-AP-5592 was granted on 25-02-2026 for support logs to be sent to a processor in Manama and limited metadata to Dublin; standard clauses per DPA Schedule 2 attached.
Negative control: the legacy vendor link to a US sandbox was decommissioned in 2024 and no personal data transfer to the US is occurring or planned.
System logs indicate failed access to two CR files; sample identifiers: CR 205-44-98761 (Qudra Dunes Logistics JSC) and CR 205-44-98762 (Qudra Dunes Logistics Ventures LLC); records are internal test stubs.
Issuer workflow note: CMA reporting under Article 50 was pre-drafted; this was a tabletop exercise only and does not reflect a live disclosure obligation.
Retention controls: onboarding KYC copies are set to 5-year rolling retention; an automated purge ran 01-02-2026 and deleted 412 expired attachments, verified by hash.
Contact and next steps: queries to privacy@riyadanajm.example.sa; we will notify affected coordinators with guidance in Arabic and English by 28-02-2026.
Statement of completion: no further corrective actions are required at this time, subject to CMA/Tadawul guidance; monitoring remains in place for 90 days.
